title: BUG 1: GRIND qgen flavor-loop QED ee→μμ 1L (17 vs 18)
description: GRIND METHOD on BUG 1 from HANDOFF.md. count_diagrams_qg21(qed_model(), [:e,:e], [:mu,:mu]; loops=1) returns 17, legacy=18, qgraf golden=18. All three dedup strategies (A,B,C) agree on 16-17 — deficit is in qgen, not dedup. Use gdb on qgraf binary to generate ground-truth intermediate results, diff against Julia port at every step until first divergence.
notes:
Root cause confirmed via gdb-instrumented qgraf-grind:
- qgraf dpntro stores ALL 6 perms of [e+,e-,γ] vertex (12 total for QED2)
- Julia dpntro stores 1 sorted entry per fieldset (2 for QED2)
- _qgen_recurse uses multiset matching + sorted slot assignment → misses valid non-canonical slot orderings
- For ee→μμ 1L: Julia 16 (Burnside) vs qgraf 18; deficit on penguin (1) and box (1) topologies
- Diagnostic data in grind/: qgraf-grind.f08 (instrumented Fortran), grind_trace.txt (qgraf state per emission), julia_trace.txt (matching Julia state)
Fix: enumerate distinct perms of 'remaining' in _qgen_recurse, with multi-edge ordering check + self-loop pair check.
close_reason: Fixed via Phase 12d (qgen.jl): _qgen_recurse now enumerates all positional perms of remaining slots + applies self-loop & multi-edge filters (qgen:13921-13954). Verified by GRIND METHOD (instrumented qgraf) + 35/35 spot-checks against qgraf golden masters + full v2 test suite (25/25 pass).

title: Phase 2: qgraf canonical.jl — full per-class lex-next-permutation
description: Implement src/v2/qgraf/canonical.jl: (1) compute_equiv_classes!(state) mirroring qg21 lines 12819-12840 (vertices partitioned by (degree, xn, xg_diag)); (2) within_row_ok(state, lin, col) and cross_row_ok(state, lin) predicates for row-level lex constraints; (3) next_class_perm!(perm, classes) iterative lex-next-permutation that advances within each equivalence class independently (NOT Cartesian, NOT pairwise — qg21 labels 77/102/202/204); (4) is_canonical_full!(state) running the full permutation over classes and comparing gam(π(i),π(j)) vs gam(i,j) in row-major order with early termination on first strict inequality; (5) updates state.ngsym when a permutation is an automorphism. This is the core bug locus: the old pairwise-swap check misses multi-vertex permutations, so 2-loop φ³ counts 474 instead of 465. Ref: qgraf-4.0.6.f08:13156-13291. Ref: ALGORITHM.md §3.5. Ref: Nogueira1993 §3 Symmetry.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests: (a) EquivClass partition matches qg21 for phi3 2L sample adjacency; (b) within_row_ok / cross_row_ok correctly reject non-canonical rows; (c) next_class_perm! enumerates exactly Π k_i! permutations; (d) is_canonical_full! detects the SPECIFIC duplicate topology pair that makes 474 vs 465 (construct it explicitly from qgraf golden master dump).
close_reason: canonical.jl delivered. 35 unit tests for _lex_next!, next_class_perm!, compute_equiv_classes!, _compare_permuted_adjacency, is_canonical_full!, is_canonical_feynman all green. Full-perm check replaces pairwise-swap bug. Integrated via topology_filter.jl delegate; 32/32 diagram_gen tests green including phi3 2L=465.

title: CRITICAL: Fix MomentumSum constructor type instability
description: Architecture + Julia idiomaticity reviews both flagged: MomentumSum inner constructor (types.jl:56-67) returns Union{Nothing, Momentum, MomentumSum}. A constructor calling new() MUST return its own type. Currently: isempty(terms) returns nothing, single-term returns Momentum, else returns MomentumSum. This infects ALL momentum arithmetic (Base.:+, Base.:-) with type instability. The compiler cannot infer return types. Fix: split into pure inner constructor (always returns MomentumSum) + separate factory function momentum_sum() that does simplification and may return the union. Update Base.:+ etc to call the factory. File: src/v2/types.jl, lines 56-67.
close_reason: Split MomentumSum: pure inner constructor (always returns MomentumSum) + momentum_sum() factory. All 4 arithmetic operators updated. 78+14+23+4+4 tests pass.

B.7 is Sigma_f^R (fermion self-energy renormalized), not the B1 reduction formula. The code's formula using A0 is derived from Eq. (4.18) and documented at (B.9). Fix: change 'Eq. B.7' to 'Eq. (B.9)' in the comment at pave_eval.jl:95. Also update verbatim string to match Eq. (B.9) exactly. File: src/v2/pave_eval.jl, line 95.

title: @test_broken regression suite for known type instabilities
description:
Anchor: test/v2/ (new test_known_bugs.jl); src/v2/pair.jl:38-43; types.jl:62-71; dirac.jl
Reports: 09_test_coverage.md §"Rule 7 violation" + recommendation #2; 06_bugs.md §"Recommended @test_broken"

CLAUDE.md Rule 7: "Known bugs MUST have @test_broken regression tests." The package currently has ZERO `@test_broken` annotations (verified — `rg @test_broken test/` empty). The list of known type-instability bugs in CLAUDE.md "Known type instabilities (MUST FIX)" is accordingly NOT regression-locked. If F009 (`pair()` Union return) lands and someone later regresses it, no test fires. Same for F010 (momentum_sum factory).

Fix: add `test/v2/test_known_bugs.jl` with `@test_broken` regressions for: (a) `@test_broken pair(LI(:mu, Dim4()), LI(:nu, DimDm4())) isa Pair` — currently returns `0::Int` (closes when F009 lands). (b) `@test_broken momentum_sum([]) isa MomentumSum` — currently `nothing` (closes when F010 lands). (c) `@test_broken DiracExpr(c1,chain1) + DiracExpr(c2,chain2)` preserves spinors when chain1/chain2 differ only in spinor pair (closes when F002 lands). (d) `@test_broken contract_colour(SUNDelta(:a,:b)*SUNF(:a,:b,:c)) == 0` (closes when F003 lands). (e) `@test_broken count_chords(self_loop_topo) == nloop` (closes when F004 lands). (f) `@test_broken substitute_index(..., li_4d, li_dminus4d)` returns AlgSum zero rather than throwing (closes when F009 cascade fixes contract.jl `_subst_factor`). Rule 7 suddenly has teeth.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/09_test_coverage.md and 06_bugs.md.

title: Hoist auto enumeration + dict caches in qgraf hot path
description:
Anchor: src/v2/qgraf/audition.jl:273, 292; src/v2/qgraf/emission_amplitude.jl:67-128, 133-155; src/v2/qgraf/halfedge.jl:35
Reports: 01_knuth.md §A1; 03_carmack.md §6 + Refix #4

`count_dedup_burnside` and `count_dedup_canonical` call `enumerate_topology_automorphisms(state)` INSIDE the per-emission callback of `_foreach_emission` — but the autos depend only on the topology, not on (ps1, pmap). `count_diagrams_qg21` already hoists this correctly at audition.jl:364; the two audition functions are inconsistent. Cost: O(|S_classes|·n²) per emission instead of per topology — for φ³ 2-loop φφ→φφ ~thousands of redundant calls. Same shape elsewhere: `_conjugate_dict(model)` and `_antiq_dict(model)` are rebuilt per-emission though stable per-model; `compute_amap(state, labels)` is recomputed three times per emission (inside emission_to_amplitude → _emission_fermion_sign, build_propagators, build_vertices).

Fix: hoist `autos = enumerate_topology_automorphisms(state)` out of the per-emission callbacks in count_dedup_burnside and count_dedup_canonical (~10 LOC, mirrors count_diagrams_qg21 pattern). Hoist `_conjugate_dict` / `_antiq_dict` once per model. Cache `amap` once per emission alongside `labels`. ~25 LOC total; eliminates 3-4× redundant work in the only documented bottleneck (solve_tree_pipeline).

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/01_knuth.md and 03_carmack.md.

title: Add add!/mul_acc! in-place AlgSum kernels and use everywhere
description:
Anchor: src/v2/expr.jl:97-130; 11 call sites in dirac_trace.jl, dirac_trick.jl, expand_sp.jl, contract.jl, eps_contract.jl, interference.jl, cross_section.jl, qgraf/burnside_combine.jl
Reports: 03_carmack.md §1 + §"What I'd do this week"; 08_julia_antipatterns.md hot-loop section

Every `result = result + sub` pattern in the algebra hot paths allocates a fresh `Dict{FactorKey,Coeff}` (full Dict-copy) on every add. AlgSum.* allocates a new Dict + N×M FactorKey constructions (each one a vcat + collect + sort) per term-pair product. The dominant cost in solve_tree at Bhabha-scale and beyond is allocation, not arithmetic. ~11 sites use this anti-pattern.

Fix: provide `add!(dst::AlgSum, src::AlgSum, sign::Coeff=1//1)` and `mul_acc!(dst::AlgSum, a::AlgSum, b::AlgSum, c::Coeff=1//1)` that mutate `dst.terms` in place. Convert every `result = result + ...` accumulation to `add!(result, ...)`. Pre-`sizehint!` destination dict in `*`. Public `+`/`*` keep their pure-functional API by wrapping a single mutable accumulator. Single biggest perf win available — Carmack estimates 2-3× wall-clock reduction on solve_tree, scales worse-than-linearly at NLO. 1 day including tests.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/03_carmack.md and 08_julia_antipatterns.md.

title: Tighten VertexRule.fields and Dict{Tuple,VertexRule} key types
description:
Anchor: src/v2/rules.jl:13, 82, 87, 112; src/v2/qcd_model.jl:33; src/v2/ew_model.jl:37; src/v2/phi3_model.jl:34
Reports: 08_julia_antipatterns.md (top instability #3 + Appendix B); 04_idiomaticity.md (cheatsheet violation #3)

`VertexRule.fields::Tuple{Vararg{Symbol}}` (length-unknown) plus `vertices::Dict{Tuple, VertexRule}` (abstract `Tuple` key) means every `haskey(rules.vertices, perm)` lookup (amplitude.jl:161, channels.jl:86, loop_channels.jl:65,67, vertex_assemble.jl:75, gauge_exchange.jl:36) hits the abstract Dict path with a boxed key. Julia's specialised hash is defeated. The `(r::FeynmanRules)(fields::Tuple{Vararg{Symbol}})` callable cannot specialise per-arity. This is THE highest-impact fix in the codebase per Antipatterns review.

Fix (preferred): per-arity dict — `vertices_3::Dict{NTuple{3,Symbol}, VertexRule}` and `vertices_4::Dict{NTuple{4,Symbol}, VertexRule}`. Forces dispatch to disambiguate, matches the existing `vertex_factor(r, fields::NTuple{3,Symbol}, mu)` signature. Alternative: `Dict{Vector{Symbol}, VertexRule}` (concrete container, length is runtime). 60 LOC across 4 model files + lookup sites in amplitude.jl + qgraf/vertex_assemble.jl. Type-stabilises every vertex lookup in every model.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/08_julia_antipatterns.md and 04_idiomaticity.md.

title: Resolve momentum_sum Union{Nothing,Momentum,MomentumSum} factory
description:
Anchor: src/v2/types.jl:62-71; cascade in src/v2/qgraf/momentum.jl:31, 227; src/v2/qgraf/propagator_assemble.jl:37, 96; src/v2/amplitude.jl:139-148; src/v2/gauge_exchange.jl:32
Reports: 08_julia_antipatterns.md (top instability #1); 04_idiomaticity.md (Session-8 audit); 02_torvalds.md (P1 types.jl:62-71); 03_carmack.md (under-engineering)

`momentum_sum` factory returns `Union{Nothing, Momentum, MomentumSum}` (3-way union) for "zero / single Momentum w/ unit coeff / general sum". The inner constructor was made type-pure (Session 8 PARTIAL fix), but the factory union still propagates: `InternalEdge.momentum`, `Propagator.mom` are typed as that 3-way Union, so every routed-edge value across the qgraf port carries the union into hot paths. Documented in DESIGN.md as MUST-FIX since Session 8 — a month of "we know about this".

Fix: make `momentum_sum` always return `MomentumSum`, with the empty Vector representing zero (`MomentumSum([])`) and a 1-element Vector representing the single-Momentum case. Re-promote `InternalEdge.momentum::MomentumSum`, `Propagator.mom::MomentumSum` and the ~10 callers. Add a `Base.iszero(::MomentumSum)` and `Base.length(::MomentumSum)` for the few special cases that currently dispatch on the union. ~50 LOC cross-cutting qgraf/.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/08_julia_antipatterns.md and 04_idiomaticity.md, 02_torvalds.md, 03_carmack.md.

title: Fix pair() factory Union{Int,Pair} return (Session-8 cockroach)
description:
Anchor: src/v2/pair.jl:38-43; cascade in src/v2/contract.jl:107,118,130; src/v2/expand_sp.jl:56-93; src/v2/eps_contract.jl:75-83
Reports: 04_idiomaticity.md (top fix #1); 08_julia_antipatterns.md (top instability #2 + Appendix A); 06_bugs.md §B6; 02_torvalds.md (P1); 03_carmack.md (Pair-aliases under-engineering)

`pair(::LorentzIndex, ::LorentzIndex)` returns `Union{Int, Pair}` — `0::Int` when BMHV `dim_contract` vanishes, otherwise a `Pair`. This is the LAST unfixed Session-8 type instability and it cascades to 8 downstream `isa Number` guards in contract.jl, expand_sp.jl, eps_contract.jl, plus a structural risk in `_subst_factor` (contract.jl:184) where the 0::Int can land in a Vector{AlgFactor}-typed comprehension and crash.

Fix (Flavour A, minimal): make `pair(::LI,::LI)` always return `MetricTensor` and route BMHV-vanishing through caller-side AlgSum (e.g. return `alg(0)`). All 8 `isa Number` checks collapse to AlgSum dispatch. Flavour B (more idiomatic): route BMHV vanishing through the COEFFICIENT, not the factor — so the contraction engine never sees a zero in the factor list. Either way removes the Union, fixes B6, closes the Session-8 cockroach, removes a Rule-8 anti-pattern, and stabilises inference for `_do_contraction`, `_try_expand`, `_det4x4_pairs`. ~30 LOC, 1 hour.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/04_idiomaticity.md and 08_julia_antipatterns.md, 06_bugs.md, 02_torvalds.md, 03_carmack.md.
close_reason: pair() factory now type-stable: pair(::PairArg,::PairArg)=Pair(a,b) always returns concrete Pair (was Union{Int,Pair}). BMHV vanishing handled at the 2 callers that need it: _do_contraction(MT,MT) keeps its existing upstream dim_contract check, _det4x4_pairs gets an inline dim_contract guard. 7 dead 'isa Number' guards removed across contract.jl/expand_sp.jl/eps_contract.jl. _try_expand result type tightened from Vector{Tuple{Coeff,Union{AlgFactor,Nothing}}} to Vector{Tuple{Coeff,AlgFactor}}. _gamma_pair_to_alg helper deleted (dead). Reviewer APPROVE. Full v2 suite 1451/1451 green, zero regressions. -28 LOC net.

title: Eliminate solve_tree vs solve_tree_pipeline schism
description:
Anchor: src/v2/cross_section.jl:48-68 (solve_tree); :203-253 (solve_tree_pipeline); src/Feynfeld.jl:152
Reports: 02_torvalds.md P1; 05_architecture.md (R3, R4 + Panel A); 10_vision_match.md §"Top 10 vision-misalignment items #4"; 03_carmack.md §"under-engineering"; 04_idiomaticity.md §"export hygiene"

Two solvers — `solve_tree` (legacy, single-channel only with `# TODO: multi-channel interference (Phase B)` at line 61) and `solve_tree_pipeline` (qgraf-driven, Bhabha trace-only landed Session 32) — are both exported, take the same `CrossSectionProblem` input, but return DIFFERENT NamedTuples (`(amplitude_squared, channels, rules)` vs `(amplitude_squared, n_emissions, orbit_denoms)`). An agent picking solve_tree for Bhabha gets silently incorrect single-channel results. solve_tree also has a latent bug (cross_section.jl:54 types `all_amps::Tuple{DiracExpr,DiracExpr}[]` which crashes at push! for fermion-exchange channels, untriggered today only because the fermion-exchange tests already moved to solve_tree_pipeline).

Fix: pick one. Mark solve_tree as `@deprecated` or rename `solve_tree_legacy`; unify the return shape (e.g. a `PipelineResult` NamedTuple alias used by both); migrate the ~10 test files (test_vertical, test_pipeline, test_phase18a_pipeline, test_solve_tree_pipeline, test_phase18b1_*) to the unified shape. Once Bhabha+Compton+qq̄→gg+ee→WW all flow through solve_tree_pipeline (Phase 18b epic feynfeld-xa7s), delete solve_tree entirely. Extract solve_tree_pipeline into its own file `pipeline_solver.jl` (cross_section.jl is currently 253 LOC, violating Rule 11).

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/02_torvalds.md and 05_architecture.md, 10_vision_match.md, 03_carmack.md, 04_idiomaticity.md.

title: Tighten Gram-singular thresholds in tid.jl + pave_eval.jl
description:
Anchor: src/v2/tid.jl:126; src/v2/pave_eval.jl:89; src/v2/d_tensor.jl:48
Reports: 01_knuth.md §C3; 06_bugs.md §"Numerical fragility map" #5

The threshold `abs(det_G) < 1e-30 && error(...)` is wrong on two counts. (a) The threshold is too tight: for physical kinematics with √s ~ 1 GeV and small angles, det_G scales like `s³ sin²θ`, easily 10⁻¹² to 10⁻²⁰ before being "really" singular — well above the 1e-30 trip wire and silently inaccurate. (b) `error()` is the wrong response — LoopTools and COLLIER both fall back to a different reduction scheme (OPP / Stuart) when Gram is near-singular; throwing kills the calculation in physically-degenerate-but-not-pathological corners.

Fix: use a relative threshold `abs(det_G)/scale < 1e-12` where `scale = max(abs(g_ii))^3`, and replace `error()` with a documented fallback path (or at minimum a clear error message printing the actual scale). Same pattern at pave_eval.jl:89 and d_tensor.jl:48. P1 because it bites the moment 2→3 enters scope (box rate explodes, near-singular becomes routine).

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/01_knuth.md and 06_bugs.md.

title: Promote symbolic mass placeholder warnings to errors
description:
Anchor: src/v2/qgraf/vertex_assemble.jl:196-197; src/v2/amplitude.jl:116
Reports: 06_bugs.md §L9; 09_test_coverage.md (qgraf vertex_assemble.jl:196-197)

When a field's `mass != :zero`, the spinor placeholder mass is hard-coded to `1//1` (a unit Rational) — the actual mass symbol value is silently dropped. Massive computations therefore give numerically-wrong answers (mass = 1 instead of m_e ≈ 5e-4). This is currently labelled "Phase 18b symbolic masses" deferred work, but any user driving the pipeline today with a massive process gets nonsense numerics with no error.

Fix: until bead `feynfeld-feen` (Phase 18b-6 Symbolic mass placeholders) lands, change the placeholder code path to throw a clear `error("massive externals not supported until feynfeld-feen lands")` rather than silently produce a wrong number. This is the standard guard-rail pattern used throughout the qgraf port for deferred work.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/06_bugs.md and 09_test_coverage.md.

title: Fix SUNF antisymmetry — SUNF(a,a,c) must vanish
description:
Anchor: src/v2/colour_types.jl:55-66; src/v2/colour_simplify.jl:81-85
Reports: 06_bugs.md §B4

`SUNF(a,b,c)` is fully antisymmetric so `f^{aac} = 0`, but the constructor permits repeated indices and stores them with a meaningless sign computed via `_parity` over a non-injective permutation. The latent trigger is `_delta_substitute(d::SUNDelta, f::SUNF)` at colour_simplify.jl:81-85: when both d.a and d.b appear in f, `_replace_adj` substitutes one and leaves a SUNF with two equal indices that the engine treats as nonzero. test_colour.jl exercises canonical f·f, d·d identities but not the cross-contraction `δ^{ab}f^{abc}`.

Fix: the SUNF constructor should detect repeated indices and return zero (either via `nothing` sentinel handled at AlgSum lift, or via lifting through `safe_sunf` that absorbs into the coefficient as `alg(0)`). Add a regression test for `δ^{ab} f^{abc} == 0` and the indirect `_delta_substitute` triggering pattern.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/06_bugs.md.

title: Fix DiracExpr.simplify silently dropping spinors
description:
Anchor: src/v2/dirac_expr.jl:39-41, 93-107
Reports: 06_bugs.md §B3; 03_carmack.md §4 (dirac_expr O(N²) blowup)

`simplify(::DiracExpr)` keys the term-collection Dict on `gammas(chain)` (gamma-only filter, line 97) and rebuilds chains as `DiracChain(gs)` (line 104) with spinors gone. Two DiracExpr terms whose chains differ only in their spinor pair (e.g. `dot(ubar(p1),GA(:mu),v(p2))` vs `dot(vbar(p2),GA(:mu),u(p1))`) collide and merge — wrong answer. `Base.:+(::DiracExpr, ::DiracExpr)` calls simplify (line 40) so any user combining DiracExprs via `+` triggers the bug.

Today this is masked because `_fermion_line_chain` builds DiracExpr via the constructor directly, never via `+`. Phase 18b-2..8 (multi-orbit interference, multi-vertex fermion lines) WILL combine DiracExprs via `+` and silently lose spinors. Two-line fix: key on full `chain.elements` instead of `gammas(chain)`, OR guard `simplify` with an assertion that all terms are spinor-free. Add `@test_broken` regression with two terms differing only in spinor pair.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/06_bugs.md and 03_carmack.md.

title: Fix _qgen_recurse pmap save/restore (TODO BUG 2)
description:
Anchor: src/v2/qgraf/qgen.jl:579, 588-593
Reports: 01_knuth.md §A6; 02_torvalds.md P0 (qgen.jl:588-592); 06_bugs.md §S3; 07_smells.md §"top 10 worst offenders #3"

`_qgen_recurse` only saves neighbour slots on backtrack — `pmap[vv, rdeg+1..vdeg]` (the just-assigned slots on the current vertex) is mutated and never restored. The 5-line TODO at qgen.jl:588 admits this and argues "benign for currently-passing cases (next perm overwrites)". That argument holds only as long as every successful match writes ALL slots `rdeg+1..vdeg`; any future filter that rejects after a partial fill leaks state across recursion frames.

Fix (cheap): extend the `saved` Vector{Tuple{Int,Int,Symbol}} block at line 579 to also capture pmap[vv, rdeg+1..vdeg] before the recursive call and restore on backtrack. Add a regression test that pushes a synthetic vertex-specific filter rejecting after 50% pmap fill, to lock the invariant in. While there, also dedup `_qgen_recurse` (qgen.jl:558-612) and `_qgen_enumerate_recurse` (qgen.jl:497-556) into a single callback-based driver — they are 50-line copy/paste of each other and the bug fix in one will not propagate.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/01_knuth.md and 02_torvalds.md, 06_bugs.md, 07_smells.md.

title: Phase 18b-1c: Physical-antiparticle field labeling + dispatch fix
description: Bhabha pipeline produces ZERO physical t-channel emissions because (a) in_fields = [leg.field_name for leg in prob.incoming] drops is_antiparticle (p2 positron labeled :e same as p1 electron), and (b) _expand_external_fields's alternation heuristic happens to recover correct labels for identity ps1 only by coincidence with ordering. For non-identity ps1 (t-channel orbits), slot-to-physical field mismatch blocks correct fermion-line dispatch. Empirically verified (Session 31 diagnostic): qgen DOES enumerate 16 emissions including t-channel topology (v5=(p1,k1), v6=(p2,k2)) under physical ext_raw=[:e,:e_bar,:e_bar,:e] (no alternation). BUT _spinor_dispatch uses anti=_is_antiparticle_field(field) — so :e_bar+!incoming+anti=T incorrectly gives v(k1) for outgoing-electron k1 (should be ubar(k1)). Fix scope (~30-50 LOC): (1) cross_section.jl compute ext_raw using XOR rule: slot label is :e_bar iff (antiparticle XOR !incoming). (2) Bypass or fix _expand_external_fields so correct ext_raw survives to qgen. (3) Pass phys_antiparticle[] through to build_externals alongside physical_moms[]. (4) _spinor_dispatch takes phys_anti (not field-derived) and is_bar_field separately. With this fix, Bhabha t-channel emissions produce u(p1)+ubar(k1) at v5 — 1-vertex fermion lines — so walk_fermion_lines works UNCHANGED. This precludes needing Phase 18b-3 (feynfeld-a7f2) for Bhabha. Blocker order: Step 1 (feynfeld-ghqq) → Step 2 (this) → Step 4 (feynfeld-vjw9, orbit dedup) → Step 5 (feynfeld-rj1l, interference).
acceptance_criteria: (1) cross_section.jl derives ext_raw from (field_name, antiparticle, incoming) using XOR rule. (2) ExternalFactor carries physical antiparticle flag separately from field label. (3) _spinor_dispatch routes on (is_bar_field, phys_anti, incoming, p, m). (4) For Bhabha, orbit-2 emissions now produce (p1-k1)²=t denom (not (p1-k2)²=u). (5) walk_fermion_lines succeeds for all 16 Bhabha emissions without crashing. (6) All existing tests green. (7) New unit test: non-identity ps1 path through build_externals for Bhabha t-channel emission.
close_reason: Step 2 landed: _qgraf_ext_labels XOR helper + phys_anti threaded through _foreach_emission/emission_to_amplitude/build_externals/walk_fermion_lines. Bhabha 16 emissions now resolve fully via direct enumeration; denoms partition into s+t (acceptance #4 met). solve_tree_pipeline still gated on Step 4 (vjw9 orbit dedup) but no longer crashes on any emission. 7 new tests in test/v2/qgraf/test_phase18b1_step2_dispatch.jl. Full suite 1332 pass + 6 broken (was 1325+6, +7 new, 0 regressions). Reviewer pass: no blockers, 3 deferred concerns (semantics overload of ExternalFactor.antiparticle, Union{Nothing,...} ergonomics, count_dedup TODO marker added).

title: Phase 18b-1a: Orbit-rep dedup strategy for multi-orbit |M|²
description: Phase 18b-1 (feynfeld-ewgw) implemented the Burnside combine machinery and wired it into solve_tree_pipeline via an 'is_emission_canonical' filter (one canonical rep per orbit, weights=1). This works for single-orbit processes (ee→μμ — Phase 18a regression PASS) but fails for Bhabha ee→ee tree: is_emission_canonical returns n_emissions=1 (canonical-rep under-count bug flagged in HANDOFF Session 22 Phase 17a VERDICT — 'Strategy C: the canonical orbit-rep may be INVALID for qgen, so the orbit yields 0 emissions instead of 1'). Bhabha has 2 orbits (count_diagrams_qg21=2) but canonical filter only keeps 1. Three options for resolution: (A) fall back within is_emission_canonical to lex-next-smallest valid rep when the lex-smallest is qgen-invalid; (B) switch solve_tree_pipeline to Strategy A (all emissions × Burnside weights 1/|Orbit|) and solve the momentum-label-mismatch inside spin_sum_interference by canonicalising bar_mom per bundle before interference (relabel orbit-members to a shared reference labeling); (C) group bundles by orbit signature (hash of canonicalised pmap) post-emission and pick a qgen-valid rep per orbit. Option (A) is the targeted fix for the known Strategy-C bug; Option (B) is more general but adds relabeling complexity; Option (C) is a compromise. Blocker for Phase 18b-1 Bhabha acceptance test. Reference: HANDOFF Session 22 Phase 17a VERDICT table + Session 27 notes.
acceptance_criteria: (1) solve_tree_pipeline(Bhabha ee→ee tree) returns n_emissions=2 (one canonical rep per orbit). (2) pipeline AlgSum matches handbuilt trace-only reference (T_tt + T_ss - 2 T_int after contract+expand_sp) symbolically. (3) ee→μμ Phase 18a regression still green. (4) decision between A/B/C documented in the bead resolution notes.
notes:
Session 32 (2026-04-25, Opus 4.7 1M) — STEP 2 LANDED.

feynfeld-gpi5 closed. _qgraf_ext_labels (XOR rule, all-incoming convention) + phys_anti threading: now in cross_section.jl/audition.jl/emission_amplitude.jl/vertex_assemble.jl/fermion_line.jl. Re-evaluation per Session 31's open question: with t-channel emissions now visible, the original A/B/C strategy choice should be re-examined empirically. title: Spiral 10 Phase D: Tensor Integral Decomposition (TID)
description: The core new algorithm for Spiral 10. Given traced AlgSum with SP(q,...) terms from box interference + denominator structure, evaluate the loop integral by reducing to scalar PaVe functions. Uses PV identity 2q·k_j = D_j - D_0 + f_j to cancel propagators. Reduces rank-2 terms to rank-1 C/D integrals via propagator cancellation. ~180 LOC in src/v2/tid.jl. Depends on Phases A-C (loop_channels, loop_amplitude, loop_interference). Ref: Denner1993 Eqs (4.7)-(4.9).

title: [bug] eeZ vertex gamma5 ordering: (gV-gA*g5)*gmu vs gmu*(gV-gA*g5)

This causes growing residual in s-Z × t-ν cross-term at high energy (55% at 500 GeV). Diagonal |M_sZ|² is unaffected (gA² sign invariant). Fixing requires careful handling to avoid breaking existing tests.

title: Fix C0p2 degenerate case: a≈0 when p10≈p20 (vertex correction kinematics)
description: The Denner C0p2 formula divides by a = px1-px2 (or px3-px1), which is zero when two nonzero momenta are equal. This is the standard QED vertex correction kinematics (p10=p20=m², p12=q²). Currently falls back to slow quadgk. Fix: either derive the L'Hôpital limit for a→0, or use the FF library (ffxc0) which doesn't have this singularity. Blocked by the FF library implementation if we want a clean fix.
notes: RESOLVED BY STRATEGY CHANGE: C0p2 a≈0 degenerate case is handled correctly by the FF library (ffxc0). Using LoopTools.jl ccall eliminates this issue.
close_reason: Superseded: LoopTools.jl ccall handles all degenerate cases

title: PERF: Analytical C₀ via 't Hooft-Veltman Spence formulas (replace nested quadgk)
description: Current C₀ uses nested quadgk (1-5ms per call, 1000x slower than necessary). The 't Hooft-Veltman 1979 analytical formula gives C₀ in terms of 2-4 dilogarithm (Li₂) evaluations — ~1-10μs. LoopTools (refs/LoopTools/src/C/) implements this with case dispatch: C0p0 (all momenta zero, pure logs), C0p1/C0p2/C0p3 (1/2/3 nonzero momenta, Spence formulas), C0soft/C0coll (IR/collinear limits). Julia already has PolyLog.jl with li2. Port the analytical formulas, dispatch on kinematic case. ~200-400 LOC. This alone would cut test_vertex_g2 from 8min to <1sec. Ref: refs/papers/tHooftVeltman1979_NuclPhysB153.pdf Eqs (5.2)-(5.30). Ref: refs/LoopTools/src/C/C0func.F.
notes: PARTIALLY COMPLETE: C0p0 (all momenta zero) is analytically solved. For general C₀, switching to LoopTools.jl ccall (FF library). Native Julia analytical C₀ deferred to future work.
close_reason: C₀ evaluation complete: C0p0 analytical, general via COLLIER ccall. 100,000× speedup for non-degenerate cases.

title: HIGH: Implement gamma5 traces

Currently dirac_trace.jl only handles chains of gamma^mu and gamma.p — no gamma5, no gamma6/7 projectors. Must implement: Tr[γ5 γ^a γ^b γ^c γ^d] = 4i ε^{abcd} (the Levi-Civita trace formula). title: HIGH: Add B₀ imaginary part analytical comparison tests

title: HIGH: Fix inconsistent iε sign convention
description: Julia idiomaticity review flagged: schwinger.jl uses complex(f, +1e-30) while pave_eval.jl uses complex(f, -1e-30) for the iε prescription. Both implement the Feynman iε = +iε in the propagator (1/(p²-m²+iε)), but the sign in the logarithm argument depends on whether you're in the numerator or denominator. These must be consistent: the physical convention is m² → m² - iε (mass² gets negative imaginary part), so ln(m²) → ln(m² - iε) = ln(|m²|) - iπθ(-m²). Audit both files and ensure a consistent sign convention documented in a comment. Files: src/v2/schwinger.jl, src/v2/pave_eval.jl.

title: CRITICAL: Fix DiracExpr.+ unbounded term growth
description: Julia idiomaticity + architecture reviews both flagged: dirac_expr.jl:36-38 Base.:+(a::DiracExpr, b::DiracExpr) = DiracExpr(vcat(a.terms, b.terms)) — blindly concatenates without collecting like terms. Unlike AlgSum (which uses Dict for O(1) collection), DiracExpr.+ causes O(N) duplicate accumulation after N additions. simplify(de::DiracExpr) exists (lines 90-104) but is only called at the end of dirac_trick, not after arithmetic. In dirac_trick recursion (n>=4), each call triples term count before simplification, causing exponential blowup. Fix options: (1) Use Dict{Vector{DiracGamma}, AlgSum} internally (matching AlgSum pattern), or (2) Call simplify inside Base.:+ (trades per-op overhead for bounded growth), or (3) Simplify every K=10 additions. File: src/v2/dirac_expr.jl, lines 36-38.
close_reason: Base.:+ now calls simplify() after vcat — like-term collection on every add prevents O(N²) blowup. 1-line change, 47 tests pass.

description: THE MOST ALGORITHMIC 2-point function. ~600 lines of case analysis: both masses zero, one zero, both equal, general case. Each with Taylor expansions for degenerate limits. Threshold/pseudo-threshold handling. Dual implementation (FF + Denner). Also B1, B00, B11, DB0 derivatives. Store as (finite, 1/ε, 1/ε²) triples. Ref: src/B/ffxb0.F (real), ffcb0.F (complex), Bcoeff.F, BcoeffFF.F, BcoeffAD.F.
close_reason: Done: _B0 via QuadGK Feynman parameter in src/v2/pave_eval.jl, all special cases tested

title: Implement dilogarithm Li2 (real and complex)
description: Port LoopTools ffxli2.F (real) and ffcli2.F (complex). The atomic building block — all C0/D0 integrals reduce to Li2 combinations. Uses Bernoulli-number series expansion, careful branch-cut handling, eta function for complex arguments. Also: Li2omx (Li2(1-x) for accuracy near x=1), Spence function, auxCD utilities. Ref: src/util/ffxli2.F, ffcli2.F, Li2.F, Li2omx.F, auxCD.F.
close_reason: Done: using PolyLog.jl (li2), no reimplementation needed

title: Implement A0 (scalar 1-point integral)
description: Trivial exact result: A0(m) = m*(1 - log(m/μ²) + δ) where δ = 2/(4-D) - γ_E + log(4π) in MSbar. Also A00. Ref: src/A/Aget.F, ffxa0.F.
close_reason: Done: _eval_A in src/v2/pave_eval.jl, tested in test_pave.jl

title: Phase 2: Numerical Loop Integrals (LoopTools port)
description: Port LoopTools' numerical evaluation of scalar 1-loop integrals to native Julia. Dual-implementation architecture (FF + Denner) for cross-validation. Atomic building block is the dilogarithm Li2. Must match LoopTools to machine precision.
close_reason: v1-era epic superseded by PRD v0.2 spiral methodology. C0/D0/validation tracked as individual issues.

title: Validate Phase 1d: translate LoopIntegrals MUnit tests (symbolic)
description: Translate relevant FeynCalc Tests/LoopIntegrals/*.test for symbolic PaVe operations. 122 test files total — prioritise PaVe.test, PaVeReduce.test, TID.test, Tdec.test.
close_reason: Phase 1d validated: 11 tests for Tdec rank 0/1/2, PaVeReduce B-functions. 506 total tests.

title: Implement Tdec (tensor decomposition)
description: Port FeynCalc Tdec. General tensor decomposition using projection methods. Uses Alexey Pak's symmetrization algorithm. TIDL lookup for pre-computed results. Ref: LoopIntegrals/Tdec.m, Tables/TIDL.m.
close_reason: Tdec rank 1/2 in tdec.jl: metric term + external momentum terms. Higher ranks deferred.

title: Implement PaVeReduce
description: Port FeynCalc PaVeReduce. Reduces PaVe coefficient functions to scalar A0, B0, C0, D0. Options: BReduce, A0ToB0, $LimitTo4. Ref: LoopIntegrals/PaVeReduce.m.
close_reason: PaVeReduce in pave_reduce.jl: B1/B00/B11 reduction to A0+B0. C/D reductions deferred to Phase 2.

title: Implement FeynAmpDenominator and propagator types
description: Port FeynCalc FeynAmpDenominator wrapping propagator denominators. PropagatorDenominator[Momentum[q],m] = 1/(q²-m²). StandardPropagatorDenominator (modern form). CartesianPropagatorDenominator. These are symbolic — no numerical evaluation yet. Ref: SharedObjects.m FeynAmpDenominator section.

title: Implement TID (tensor integral decomposition)
description: Port FeynCalc TID. Takes TID[amp, q] where q is loop momentum. Decomposes tensor loop integrals into scalar integrals via Passarino-Veltman decomposition. Solves linear system for coefficients. UsePaVeBasis option. Handles vanishing Gram determinants. Ref: LoopIntegrals/TID.m.

title: Implement PaVe type system (A0, B0, B1, C0, D0, GenPaVe)
description: Refine current PaVe types. FeynCalc PaVe[i,j,...,{p²},{m²}] with index symmetry and auto-reduction to named scalars. GenPaVe uses different momentum convention. Ref: LoopIntegrals/PaVe.m, SharedObjects.m PaVe section.

description: Port FeynCalc's PaVe coefficient function symbols, FeynAmpDenominator propagator types, TID (tensor integral decomposition), Tdec (tensor decomposition), PaVeReduce. These are the symbolic loop integral layer — numerical evaluation comes in Phase 2.

title: Centralise _sp_key (3 inline copies)
description:
Anchor: src/v2/sp_context.jl:12; src/v2/polarization_sum.jl:55; src/v2/sp_lookup.jl:6
Reports: 07_smells.md §"Duplication"

`_sp_key` (sort a 2-tuple symbol/symbol pair canonically) is implemented three times: the canonical version in sp_context.jl:12, plus inline duplicates in polarization_sum.jl:55 (`key = a.name <= b.name ? (a.name,b.name) : (b.name,a.name)`) and sp_lookup.jl:6 (`key = a <= b ? (a, b) : (b, a)`). Pure drift hazard — three copies will diverge under any future change to canonicalisation rules.

Fix: export `_sp_key` from sp_context.jl; replace inlined logic in polarization_sum.jl:55 (`key = _sp_key(a.name, b.name)`) and sp_lookup.jl:6 (`key = _sp_key(a, b)`). 6 LOC saved + drift hazard eliminated.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/07_smells.md.
close_reason: Centralised _sp_key — replaced 2 inline duplicates in polarization_sum.jl:55 and sp_lookup.jl:6 with calls to _sp_key from sp_context.jl. Full suite 1451/1451 green, zero regressions.

title: Numerator returns Coeff (not Any/Number) in _do_contraction + _eps_replace_slot
description:
Anchor: src/v2/contract.jl:107, 118, 130, 142-143, 153-154, 161-173
Reports: 08_julia_antipatterns.md (entries #5, #13)

Two related Union-return cleanups in contract.jl: (a) `_do_contraction` returns `Union{Nothing, Tuple{Any, Vector{AlgFactor}}}` because the first slot is sometimes `0` (Int), `1` (Int), `Number`, `Pair` mixed. The downstream `new_coeff, new_factors = result` line then has to splat-dispatch a tuple whose first element type is `Any`. (b) `_eps_replace_slot` returns `Union{Nothing, Symbol, Eps}` — `nothing` for "not found", the literal `Symbol :vanishes` for antisymmetric, otherwise `Eps`. Caller (`_do_contraction` lines 142, 153) must triple-check.

Fix: (a) coerce the first tuple slot to `Coeff` (`Rational{Int}(0)`, `1//1`, etc.) so the tuple is `Tuple{Coeff, Vector{AlgFactor}}` always — pure Rule-8 fix. (b) replace the `:vanishes` Symbol sentinel with either two-method dispatch (`_replace_slot_or_zero`) or a small enum / Maybe-style struct. Removes a 3-way Union and an `isa Symbol` check. Note F009 (pair() Union fix) eliminates much of (a) at the source.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/08_julia_antipatterns.md.

title: Pipeline-equivalent for Schwinger / vertex F2(0) / running α / ee→WW Grozin
description:
Anchor: src/v2/schwinger.jl:14-19; src/v2/vertex.jl:31-35; src/v2/running_alpha.jl:23+; src/v2/ew_cross_section.jl:24+
Reports: 10_vision_match.md (Panel B + #3); 05_architecture.md (R8 + Pipeline coverage matrix)

Four files explicitly self-identify as bypasses with the comment "REFERENCE IMPLEMENTATION — validates physics formula directly. The pipeline should reproduce this result via Model → Rules → Diagrams → Algebra → Integrals → Evaluate. Do NOT delete: used for cross-validation against pipeline results." For these to actually be cross-validations, the OTHER side of the cross — the pipeline implementation — must exist. None do. The cross-validation is currently between a recipe and itself (pinned numerics). The disclaimers have been in place 7+ sessions without redemption.

Fix: write pipeline counterparts via solve_tree_pipeline / nlo_box / future loop infrastructure: (a) Schwinger correction = Born × (1+δ) emerging from solve_nlo(ee→μμ) once IR subtraction infrastructure exists (multi-spiral effort, gated on Spiral 11+ work). (b) Vertex F₂(0) at the box-diagram level: writable today via existing PaVe + nlo_box machinery for the on-shell vertex form factor. (c) Running α(q²) — the Π̂_f vacuum-polarization pipeline counterpart for one fermion species (then sum the SM table from outside). (d) ee→WW σ via solve_tree_pipeline once chiral vertex / γ⁵ traces / Eps contraction (existing bead feynfeld-qu1) all stabilize. Move the four reference files into a `src/v2/references/` subdirectory and create a `Feynfeld.References` namespace, OR keep the files but flip the test direction once each pipeline counterpart lands. Promote bead `feynfeld-cur` (currently P2) to P1.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/10_vision_match.md and 05_architecture.md.

title: Add Base.hash(::AlgSum) + canonicalize MomentumSum constructor
description:
Anchor: src/v2/expr.jl:82; src/v2/types.jl:53-59, 74-75
Reports: 04_idiomaticity.md (top-fix #4 + hash audit); 06_bugs.md §B5; 08_julia_antipatterns.md

Two hash/== contract gaps: (a) `AlgSum` defines `==` (expr.jl:82) but no explicit `Base.hash` — relies on Julia's default field-hashing, which works correctly via Dict.hash but is slow and breaks if AlgSum ever lands in a Set or Dict-key position. (b) `MomentumSum` inner constructor (types.jl:53-59) accepts whatever ordering is passed and stores it raw; `Base.:(==)` and `Base.hash` compare/hash the terms array directly, so two logically-equal sums with different sort order are unequal. Today every caller writes terms in lex order of momentum names (`p1` before `p2`) so the bug is dormant; one new caller swapping order silently breaks `==`. Documented in DESIGN.md as MUST-FIX.

Fix: (a) `Base.hash(s::AlgSum, h::UInt) = hash(s.terms, hash(:AlgSum, h))` — one line. (b) MomentumSum inner constructor should sort terms canonically (and dedup like-terms via the existing `momentum_sum` factory logic, OR delegate to the factory). Add `@test_broken MomentumSum([(2//1,p1),(1//1,p2)]) == MomentumSum([(1//1,p2),(2//1,p1)])` regression. Also flag for AlgSum.show: dict-iteration order is non-deterministic; sort terms in show output for reproducible test repr.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/04_idiomaticity.md and 06_bugs.md, 08_julia_antipatterns.md.

title: Fold _eval_A/_B/_C/_D type-letter helpers + naming consistency
description:
Anchor: src/v2/pave_eval.jl:14-17, 27, 44, 78, 101; src/v2/b0_eval.jl:11, 112; src/v2/d_tensor.jl:16
Reports: 04_idiomaticity.md (top-fix #2 + Type-in-function-name audit); 02_torvalds.md "Naming sins"

Private helpers `_eval_A(::PaVe{1})`, `_eval_B(::PaVe{2})`, `_eval_B_tensor`, `_eval_C(::PaVe{3})`, `_eval_C_tensor`, `_eval_D(::PaVe{4})`, `_eval_D_tensor` smuggle in the type-in-function-name pattern the cheatsheet bans (cheatsheet: "✗ evaluate_pave(x) → ✓ evaluate(x::PaVe)"). The names encode the type parameter N (A=1, B=2, C=3, D=4) which is already known from dispatch on PaVe{N}. While there: a few other naming consistencies — `dot` exported as Dirac chain builder (collides with LinearAlgebra; covered in F027), `gammas` as exported noun-verb extractor (collides with any user `gammas` variable), `evaluate*` family with 8 different suffixes (`_m_squared`, `_numeric`, `_box_integral`, `_sp`, etc.) — pick one discipline (always suffix or always pure dispatch).

Fix: rename to non-type names — `_eval_scalar(pv::PaVe{1};...)`, `_eval_scalar(pv::PaVe{2};...)`, `_eval_tensor(pv::PaVe{2};...)`, `_eval_tensor(pv::PaVe{3};...)` etc. ~20 LOC; the public `evaluate(::PaVe{N})` API is unchanged. Optionally fold inline. While there: rename `is_canonical_full!` / `is_canonical_qgraf!` / `is_canonical_feynman` to algorithm-revealing names (`is_canonical_smallest!`, `is_canonical_largest!`, `is_canonical_legacy_bridge`).

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/04_idiomaticity.md and 02_torvalds.md.

title: Remove getproperty legacy accessors + ExternalLeg back-compat shim

title: Layer-2 unification — move triple_gauge_vertex + EW chiral constants
description:
Anchor: src/v2/qcd_model.jl:50-65; src/v2/ew_parameters.jl:30-35; src/v2/rules.jl:30-34; src/v2/gauge_exchange.jl:35
Reports: 05_architecture.md (R7, R2 + "QCDModel and EWModel — Layer 1 or Layer 2 leakage")

Layer-2 (Rules) is leaky in two ways: (a) `triple_gauge_vertex(mu1,mu2,mu3,p1,p2,p3) → AlgSum` — the V_{μ₁μ₂μ₃} Lorentz structure for the 3-gluon vertex — lives in qcd_model.jl (Layer 1c), and gauge_exchange.jl:35 calls it directly bypassing `feynman_rules`. `vertex_factor` in rules.jl:94-99 only handles 3-vertices with fermion+fermion+boson signatures; 3-boson vertices fall through and callers reach into qcd_model.jl. EW WWγ/WWZ vertices have no `vertex_structure` method either — test_pipeline.jl:344-352 calls `triple_gauge_vertex` from QCD's file then patches the coupling phase. (b) `EW_GV_E_R, EW_GA_E_R` (Rational chiral coupling constants) live in ew_parameters.jl (Layer 5 neighborhood) but `rules.jl:30-34` references them — works only because `vertex_structure(::Val{:e_Z}, ...)` resolves them lazily at first call, not at top-level evaluation of rules.jl. Any future precompiled vertex table breaks.

Fix: extract a `vertex_lorentz.jl` (Layer 2 helper) containing `triple_gauge_vertex` and (eventually) `fourgauge_vertex`. Add `vertex_structure(::Boson, ::Boson, ::Boson, ::Val{:g_s}, mus..., moms...)` dispatch in rules.jl so 3-boson and EW WWγ/WWZ flow through the same dispatch table. Move `EW_GV_E_R` / `EW_GA_E_R` / `EW_SIN2_W_R` / `EW_COS2_W_R` Rational constants from ew_parameters.jl into ew_model.jl (Layer 1c owns its couplings). Leave the Float64 EW constants in ew_parameters.jl. Reorder Feynfeld.jl includes for layer contiguity (R1 from architecture review).

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/05_architecture.md.

title: Trim Feynfeld export list — internals submodule + drop dot collision
description:
Anchor: src/Feynfeld.jl:91-156
Reports: 02_torvalds.md "API/export surface critique"; 04_idiomaticity.md §"Export surface critique" + top-fix #6,#8; 09_test_coverage.md §"Exports without tests"

The `export` block lists ~140-200 names. Many are internal helpers leaking only because tests reach for them: `_eval_factor`-adjacent names, `alg_from_factors`, `evaluate_box_integral`, `evaluate_single_box_channel`, `evaluate_box_channels`, `evaluate_m_squared`, `evaluate_numeric`, `gauge_coupling_phase`, `_PAVE_NAMES`, slot-type tags, `SpinorKind`/`UKind`/`VKind`/`UBarKind`/`VBarKind` (users only need u/v/ubar/vbar), `Massive`/`Massless`/`Charged`/`Neutral` trait singletons, `Coeff` type alias, `Dim4`/`DimD`/`DimDm4`, `arity`, `EW_GL_E`, `EW_GR_E`, `qed3_model` (unused), the `casimir_fund`/`casimir_adj`/`dim_fund`/`dim_adj` model-side dupes of colour_trace versions. Critically: `dot` is exported (Feynfeld.jl:106) and shadows `LinearAlgebra.dot` — anyone doing `using LinearAlgebra; using Feynfeld` gets silent shadowing.

Fix: introduce a `Feynfeld.Internals` submodule that re-exports the internals tests reach for; top-level Feynfeld exports only what users call from the REPL (target ~60-70 names from current ~140). Drop `dot` from exports OR rename to `dchain`/`dirac_chain`. Drop dead exports `arity`, `qed3_model`, `EW_GL_E`/`EW_GR_E`, `casimir_fund`/`casimir_adj`/`dim_fund`/`dim_adj`. The split also unblocks the public-vs-internal API contract (currently every refactor risks breaking unknown downstream users).

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/02_torvalds.md and 04_idiomaticity.md, 09_test_coverage.md.

title: Pin hidden conventions (metric signature, ε orientation, D=4-2ε) once
description:
Anchor: src/v2/pair.jl:1; src/v2/sp_context.jl:1; src/v2/coeff.jl:1; src/v2/eps_evaluate.jl:13-18, 57-65; src/v2/eps_contract.jl:9
Reports: 11_ground_truth_literate.md §"Hidden convention encoding"; 01_knuth.md (citation audit gaps); 06_bugs.md §B8 (eps_sign hardcoded)

Tribal knowledge unpinned anywhere in source: (a) METRIC SIGNATURE — Bjorken-Drell mostly-minus (+,-,-,-) is implicit in pair.jl, sp_context.jl, b0_eval.jl Im-sign, eps_evaluate.jl Gram det signs; never named. (b) D = 4 - 2ε — the regularization scheme is encoded in `DimPoly([0,1])` constants in coeff.jl:24-27 with no comment. (c) ε orientation — eps_evaluate.jl:13-18 declares `ε(p1,p2,k1,k2) > 0 in CM frame` as a project-chosen convention, not cited from any source; eps_evaluate.jl:57-65 errors on any process not labelled (:p1,:p2,:k1,:k2) — hard 2→2-only restriction the docs don't advertise (also Bugs B8 P2). (d) γ5 sign convention `$LeviCivitaSign = -1` mentioned in eps_contract.jl:9 but cite is to a FeynCalc internal flag, not a primary source.

Fix: add a single "Conventions" header to `src/v2/DESIGN.md` (or a dedicated `src/v2/CONVENTIONS.md`) listing all conventions with verbatim citations from MBD Eq. (2.1), Denner §2 (notation), MBD Eqs. (2.5-2.7) for chiral projectors, Denner Eq. (4.21) for D = 4-2ε. Then add a 3-line pointer-reference at the top of each affected file (pair.jl, sp_context.jl, coeff.jl, eps_evaluate.jl, eps_contract.jl, dirac_trace.jl). For eps_evaluate.jl:57-65 specifically, add an explicit WARNING that the sign convention is hardcoded to (:p1,:p2,:k1,:k2) labels and Phase-19 needs to generalize via a physical oriented-frame.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/11_ground_truth_literate.md and 01_knuth.md, 06_bugs.md.

title: Cite missing physics references — verbatim equations + acquire papers
description:
Anchor: src/v2/b0_eval.jl:73; pave_eval.jl:67-77; running_alpha.jl:11-12; tid.jl:8-10; loop_amplitude.jl:8-12; ew_cross_section.jl:51-78; vertex.jl:3; spin_sum.jl:1, 138-139; dirac_trace.jl:60, 107
Reports: 01_knuth.md §"Citation audit"; 11_ground_truth_literate.md §"Rule 1 violations" + §"Top 20 fixes"

Rule 2 (triple citation: path + eq number + verbatim equation) is followed in physics-bearing files but slips at multiple sites. (a) Verbatim missing where citation exists: `b0_eval.jl:73` (Källén imaginary part — bare "derived"), `pave_eval.jl:67-77` (PV identity for C₁/C₂ — derived but no verbatim), `running_alpha.jl:11-12` (Π̂_f bare "P&S Ch. 7"), `tid.jl:8-10` (vague Denner §4), `loop_amplitude.jl:8-12` (Eq 4.1 paraphrased), `dirac_trace.jl:60` (MBD Eq. 2.14 trace recursion uncited), `dirac_trace.jl:107` cites MBD (2.18) for the 4-γ γ5 trace where the explicit identity is Eq. (2.19) — minor mis-cite. (b) `spin_sum.jl` has zero `# Ref:` lines; the chiral-projector relabeling (γ⁰ P_L γ⁰ = P_R) and `_conj_gamma5_sign` are uncited QFT identities. (c) `ew_cross_section.jl:51-78` Grozin polynomial is a black-box port — Grozin "Using REDUCE in HEP" not in `refs/papers/`. (d) Schwinger 1948 cited at vertex.jl:3 not in refs/papers/. (e) COLLIER paper (arXiv:1604.06792) is in refs/COLLIER/ as the library but the paper PDF is not in refs/papers/.

Fix: one citation-discipline commit transcribing the verbatim equations from already-stored papers (Denner1993, MBD, P&S — though P&S .djvu is unreadable; cross-check via FeynCalc El-GaEl.m as per existing convention). Acquire the three missing papers via WebFetch/TIB+playwright per CLAUDE.md retrieval priority: Schwinger 1948 (APS), Grozin REDUCE (or arXiv hep-ph/9706507), COLLIER arXiv:1604.06792. Annotate `tid.jl:97-155` rank-2 PV cancellation as "Feynfeld-original algorithm" with a banner (the rank-2 trick is novel composition, not a Denner formula). Annotate `qgraf/burnside_combine.jl`, `vertex_assemble.jl`, `emission_amplitude.jl`, `fermion_line.jl`, `propagator_assemble.jl` with `# Source: Feynfeld-original (no qgraf-Fortran counterpart)` so the absence of pointer-citation is intentional rather than missing.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/01_knuth.md and 11_ground_truth_literate.md.

description:
Anchor: src/v2/DESIGN.md; Feynfeld_PRD.md; CLAUDE.md; SPIRAL_9_PLAN.md; src/v2/VERTICAL_PLAN.md; JULIA_PATTERNS.md
Reports: 07_smells.md §"Doc drift"; 10_vision_match.md §"Doc consistency"; 04_idiomaticity.md (Session-8 audit)

title: Split the 200-LOC violators (qgraf giants + cross_section + others)
description:
Anchor: src/v2/qgraf/topology.jl (744); qgraf/qgen.jl (612); qgraf/audition.jl (445); qgraf/canonical.jl (413); cross_section.jl (253); qgraf/momentum.jl (235); qgraf/filters.jl (234); contract.jl (212); qgraf/vertex_assemble.jl (210)
Reports: 02_torvalds.md "Files that should not be one file"; 05_architecture.md (Appendix B); 07_smells.md §"200-LOC violators"

Nine source files violate Rule 11 (max ~200 LOC). Six are in qgraf/. The 744-LOC topology.jl houses two giant `@label/@goto` state machines (step_b_enumerate! 205 LOC, step_c_enumerate! 312 LOC + 49 gotos across the file). DESIGN.md still claims "all files < 200 LOC" — doc drift. The natural splits are documented in the architecture review:
- topology.jl → step_b.jl, step_c.jl, qg10.jl/qg21.jl driver shells, lex_perm.jl
- qgen.jl → dpntro.jl, multiset.jl, qgen_recurse.jl, qdis.jl
- audition.jl → audit_burnside/canonical/prefilter (or just dedup.jl + emissions.jl after F020 cull)
- canonical.jl → equiv_classes.jl, class_perm.jl, canonical_predicates.jl
- cross_section.jl → kinematics.jl + pipeline_solver.jl (R3 from architecture, also resolves F008)
- filters.jl → filters_inline / filters_bridge / filters_vertex (sharing the parametric BFS from F021)

Fix: do the splits incrementally as part of finishing each consumer's spiral (e.g. cross_section.jl split lands with F008). Defer topology.jl + qgen.jl until Phase 18b is closed (highest churn). Add a CI hook that fails on any *.jl > 250 LOC after the cleanup. While there: also fold the four <50-LOC false-partitions (diagrams.jl 22, gauge_exchange.jl 38, ew_parameters.jl 39, d0_collier.jl 41).

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/02_torvalds.md and 05_architecture.md, 07_smells.md.

title: Dedup three Knuth Algorithm L lex-next implementations
description:
Anchor: src/v2/qgraf/canonical.jl:64-89; src/v2/qgraf/canonical.jl:106-134; src/v2/qgraf/topology.jl:379-404; src/v2/qgraf/audition.jl:248-253, 217-257
Reports: 02_torvalds.md (P0 canonical.jl 70-89 vs 106-134 vs topology.jl 379-404); 07_smells.md §"Duplication"; 01_knuth.md §A3 (inline _inv_perm)

Three implementations of Knuth Algorithm L (in-place lexicographic next-permutation) in the same submodule: `_lex_next!` (canonical.jl:70-89, dead — no caller), `_lex_next_class!` (canonical.jl:106-134), `_lex_next_perm!` (topology.jl:379-404). Plus inline lex-next in audition.jl:248-253 and again in `_foreach_emission` at audition.jl:217-257. Knuth A3 also notes inline `_inv_perm` duplication in audition.jl. Pure Rule-11 code-bloat.

Fix: write Algorithm L once over `AbstractVector{<:Integer}` with an index-mapping callable (or just keep `_lex_next_perm!` as canonical), delete the others, replace audition inline blocks with `_lex_next_perm!(ps1) || break`. ~50 LOC saved, removes a maintenance hazard. While there: parameterise the three BFS variants in qgraf (`_bfs_reachable`, `_is_connected_internal`, `_bfs_skip_vertex`) on a `should_skip(u,v)::Bool` predicate — same structural unification, ~60 LOC saved.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/02_torvalds.md and 07_smells.md, 01_knuth.md.

title: Consolidate three audition dedup strategies into one
description:
Anchor: src/v2/qgraf/audition.jl:266-444; src/v2/qgraf/QgrafPort.jl:51
Reports: 02_torvalds.md (P1 audition.jl); 05_architecture.md (R11)

`audition.jl` (445 LOC) houses three diagram-counting strategies (Burnside / canonical / prefilter) — the docstring at line 14 advertises them as A/B/C and "all three should agree on every diagram count". The bake-off has been won: `is_emission_canonical` (B, qgraf-faithful) is the production path. Yet `count_dedup_burnside`, `count_dedup_canonical`, `count_dedup_prefilter` are still loaded, exported (QgrafPort.jl:51), and exercised by tests (test_phase17_audition.jl runs all three on every commit, dominating the slow tail of CI runtime).

Fix: keep `count_diagrams_qg21` as the production counter; move count_dedup_burnside / count_dedup_canonical / count_dedup_prefilter to a single `test/v2/qgraf/audit_agreement.jl` that asserts all three agree on a sentinel set of processes (one per pair). Drop them from the QgrafPort exports. Delete `is_pmap_canonical` / `pmap_stabilizer` back-compat aliases at audition.jl:200-203. ~150 LOC saved + test runtime win.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/02_torvalds.md and 05_architecture.md.

title: Add @compile_workload + drop dead deps (TensorGR, PolyLog)
description:
Anchor: Project.toml; src/Feynfeld.jl
Reports: 03_carmack.md §"Dependencies" + "What I'd do this week" #2,#6; 07_smells.md §"Configuration drift"; 10_vision_match.md §"Top 10 #10" Panel E

Project.toml declares `TensorGR` and `PolyLog`. `TensorGR` is never `using`-ed anywhere in src/v2/; PRD §1.3 says "the bridge is a future spiral, not a dependency". `PolyLog` is declared and used to be planned for `running_alpha`'s Li₂ but the running-alpha file uses analytical formulas, not Li₂; current source has no `using PolyLog`. Both are pure cost on `]instantiate` for fresh clones. Separately, there is no `precompile()` call or `PrecompileTools.@compile_workload` anywhere — every fresh Julia process pays the full type-inference cost for `AlgSum +` and 50 separate test-file include startup.

Fix: (a) remove `TensorGR` and `PolyLog` from Project.toml + drop their compat entries (5 minutes); add a comment in Project.toml if either is genuinely a "reserved UUID for future bridge". (b) add a minimal `@compile_workload` block in src/Feynfeld.jl exercising `solve_tree(qed_model(), ee→μμ)`, `solve_tree_pipeline(...)`, and `count_diagrams_qg21(qed_model(), [:e,:e],[:mu,:mu]; loops=0)`. ~half a day for (b); huge TTFX win, also slashes test-suite startup cost.

title: Pack _vertex_sig / _diagram_sig into UInt64
description:
Anchor: src/v2/qgraf/audition.jl:23-48
Reports: 01_knuth.md §A2; 03_carmack.md §6

`_vertex_sig` allocates `Vector{Tuple{Int8,Symbol}}` of length vdeg_v per vertex and sorts; `_diagram_sig` allocates `Vector{Vector{Tuple{Int8,Symbol}}}` of length n. Called once per non-identity automorphism in `is_emission_canonical`, `same_emission_orbit`, `emission_stabilizer`. For |Aut|=8 and n=6 vertices, ~48 small-vector allocations PER EMISSION. Test runtime is dominated by qgraf in multi-orbit cases.

Fix: pack each (perm-target, field-symbol-id) into a UInt64 from an interned-symbol table; vertex signature becomes a sorted `NTuple{d, UInt64}` — no allocation, fast hash, fast equality. Pre-build inverse permutation table once per emission and reuse across is_emission_canonical / _diagram_sig / emission_stabilizer. Make _vertex_sig write into a caller-owned buffer. While there: dedup `_inv_perm` (audition.jl:42-47 inline vs :52-59 public) into one call. ~1 day, deepest hot spot in qgraf testing.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/01_knuth.md and 03_carmack.md.

title: Worklist contraction in contract.jl + colour_simplify.jl
description:
Anchor: src/v2/contract.jl:14-56; src/v2/colour_simplify.jl:18-69
Reports: 01_knuth.md §A8; 03_carmack.md §5

`_contract_factors` uses the `while changed; for ...; break` anti-pattern: each contraction restarts the entire scan from scratch and rebuilds `_index_inventory` (fresh Dict + walk every factor). For k Lorentz indices this is O(k³) when O(k log k) is achievable. colour_simplify.jl:18-69 has THREE nested copies of the same anti-pattern (delta self-contraction, delta substitution, structure-constant contraction). For current test sizes (k≤12) the absolute cost is OK; at 4-leg 1-loop with PaVe tensor decomposition, k routinely reaches 30+ and the asymptotic shape becomes the wall.

Fix: maintain `indices::Dict{LorentzIndex, Vector{Int}}` as a mutable structure; when a rewrite produces new factors, UPDATE the inventory incrementally (remove contracted factor's entries, add new entries for survivors), then push affected indices onto a worklist. SymbolicUtils / FORM pattern. ~2 days; the right shape for any index-contraction kernel and ROI compounds with every future expansion.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/01_knuth.md and 03_carmack.md.

title: Pre-allocate buffers in dirac_trace + dirac_trick recursion
description:
Anchor: src/v2/dirac_trace.jl:50-72, 132-153; src/v2/dirac_trick.jl:32-186, 71-186
Reports: 01_knuth.md §A9; 03_carmack.md §3 + §4

`_trace_no_g5` and `_trace_with_g5_pure` recursion build `rest = [gs[i] for i in 2:n if i != k]` per `k` per recursion level — fresh `Vector{DiracGamma}` of length n-2 per pairing. For n=8 (typical 1-loop 4-leg integrand) this is ~10⁴ small-vector allocations. `_trick_gammas` does the same with three slice allocations per find-pair iteration plus repeated `DiracGamma[left; inner; right]` concatenations.

Fix: pass a single mutable `Vector{DiracGamma}` working buffer through the recursion; remove element k via swap-with-end + truncate before recursive call, restore on return (same idiom as qgen.jl's `saved` backtrack). Combined with F012 add!, cuts constant factor 2-3× on long traces, decisively when n ≥ 12 (box×box interference). One week including correctness tests. P0 at NLO scale.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/01_knuth.md and 03_carmack.md.

title: Cache hash on FactorKey + small-N tuple specialization
description:
Anchor: src/v2/expr.jl:41-51
Reports: 03_carmack.md §2

`FactorKey` is constructed in every AlgSum `*` term-pair, every `expand_scalar_product`, every `contract`. Each construction does `sort(collect(...))` + heap allocation; `Base.hash(fk::FactorKey, h::UInt)` then iterates the Vector again on every Dict probe. P1 today, P0 at 1-loop scale.

Fix: store a precomputed `hash::UInt` field on the immutable struct (computed once at construction). For small N (most QED 2→2 trees have ≤ 4 factors), pack to `NTuple{N, AlgFactor}` in a parametric `FactorKey{N}` — `hash(::Tuple)` is unrolled, no Vector indirection. Promote to `Vector{AlgFactor}` past N=8. Optionally precompute the sort key during construction so `_factor_isless` doesn't recurse on type tags. Half a day with bench validation; 5-10% reduction on AlgSum hot path, scaling to 3-5× on dict ops.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/03_carmack.md.

title: Float64 strict-equality branches in pave_eval/b0_eval are brittle
description:
Anchor: src/v2/pave_eval.jl:29; src/v2/b0_eval.jl:18, 22, 24, 36, 60, 73, 115
Reports: 06_bugs.md §B9 + §"Numerical fragility map" 1-3,8; 01_knuth.md §C6

Strict-equality dispatch on Float64 (`m == 0.0`, `p2 == 0.0`) at multiple sites in PaVe / B0 evaluation. Kinematics obtained from arithmetic (e.g. `cosθ - cos(0)`) instead of constructed as exact `0.0` literals will miss the special-case branch and fall through to the general formula on what is logically a threshold case — loss of precision or NaN. b0_eval.jl:73 (Källén imaginary part) has a related discontinuity at p²=m² threshold. b0_eval.jl:115 errors instead of using a tolerance.

Fix: replace `m == 0.0` with `abs(m) < eps()*max(...)` style tolerance checks at all sites listed above. Add tests at the boundary (e.g. `_B0(eps_machine, m02, m12) ≈ _B0(0.0, m02, m12)` to machine precision) plus a phase-space-integrator regression that crosses s=0.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/06_bugs.md and 01_knuth.md.

title: Fix count_chords double-count on self-loops
description:
Anchor: src/v2/qgraf/spanning_tree.jl:65-67
Reports: 06_bugs.md §B2

`chord_count += Int(state.xg[i, i])` adds the half-edge count rather than the self-loop chord count. The qgraf convention (correctly noted in the inline comment "xg[i,i] = 2× self-loop count") means this overcounts by exactly 2× per self-loop. `route_momenta` correctly does `÷ 2` at momentum.jl:101 — only the diagnostic `count_chords` is wrong, so the routing code is consistent but Euler-invariant tests on self-loop topologies will misreport.

Fix: `chord_count += Int(state.xg[i, i]) ÷ 2` at both sites. Add `@test_broken count_chords(self_loop_topo) == nloop` regression.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/06_bugs.md.

After: qgraf_ext_moms[i]=physical_moms[Int(ps1[i])], ext_signs[i]=phys_idx>n_inco ? -1 : 1, threaded through build_externals (ps1-based mom lookup + incoming flag) and walk_fermion_lines. title: Symbolic propagator-denominator factor (InverseSP) in AlgSum
description: Introduce a symbolic inverse-scalar-product factor so that AlgSum can represent propagator denominators 1/(p²-m²) natively. Currently no way to carry 1/pair(q,q) through the algebra — Phase 18b-1 worked around this by returning a trace-only AlgSum plus separate per-orbit denoms (Option A), letting the downstream evaluator apply 1/denom at the numeric stage. That is pragmatic but splits the true |M|² across two representations. 'Best in class' shape: solve_tree_pipeline returns a single AlgSum that IS the physical |M|² including inverse denominators, fully symbolic. Required changes (scoped during research): (1) new factor type InverseSP wrapping a Pair{Momentum,Momentum} (or more general InverseFactor{F<:AlgFactor}); (2) add to AlgFactor union in expr.jl; implement _factor_type_tag, isless, hash, ==; (3) cancellation rule: pair(q,q) × inv(pair(q,q)) = 1 in constructor canonicalisation; (4) evaluate_sp dispatch for InverseSP (numeric 1/value); (5) contract.jl interaction (inverse factors are scalars w.r.t. Lorentz contraction — should pass through unchanged); (6) expand_sp does not cross inverse factors; (7) solve_tree_pipeline caller changes to multiply in 1/denom_i·denom_j per pair. Retrofit Phase 18b-1's multi-orbit Burnside test to compare against the full physical |M|² instead of trace-only. Reference: DESIGN.md §'How to Add a New Physics Space' (lines 197-207) — the pattern is proven for colour factors. Ref: HANDOFF Session 27 Phase 18b-1 proposal, Option B section.
acceptance_criteria: (1) InverseSP factor type integrated with AlgFactor union + isless/hash/==. (2) Cancellation pair(q,q) × inv(pair(q,q)) → 1 works in AlgSum constructor. (3) evaluate_sp(1 / pair(q,q); ctx) returns 1 / numeric(pair(q,q); ctx). (4) solve_tree_pipeline(Bhabha) returns an AlgSum that equals the full handbuilt formula (1/4)(T_tt/t² + T_ss/s² - 2 T_int/(s·t)) symbolically. (5) contract + expand_sp pass InverseSP through unchanged. (6) Rule 11: no source file over ~200 LOC. (7) Reviewer agent at end.
notes: Do NOT block on Phase 18b-1; 18b-1 goes with Option A (denom-free trace sum) as the pragmatic scope. This bead is the best-in-class retrofit. Estimated ~200 LOC across 4-5 files. Consider doing AFTER 18b-7 (coupling assignment) since couplings and masses may both benefit from symbolic-coefficient groundwork — there may be overlap to exploit.

title: 2-loop multi-edge symmetry overcounting (φφ→φφ 2L: 474 vs 465)
description: Root cause identified: topology_enum.jl uses pairwise vertex swap for canonicalization, but qgraf checks ALL permutations within equivalence classes (ALGORITHM.md §3.5). The pairwise check misses isomorphisms at 2-loop+ where 3+ equivalent vertices exist. The overcounting (474 vs 465 = 9 extra for φφ→φφ 2L) is from duplicate topologies, NOT from the multi-edge symmetry in field_assign.jl. close_reason: 474→465 φ³ 2L overcounting FIXED. Stocktake 04: 'Phase 17 dedup bug fixed (474 → 465 via full-permutation Knuth Algorithm L per equiv class)'. Root cause was pairwise-swap canonicalization; replaced by per-class full permutation in canonical.jl. Regression test in test/v2/qgraf/test_count_diagrams_qg21.jl.

title: Performance: canonical pruning for 2-loop+ topology enumeration
description: CRITICAL: topology_enum.jl does NOT implement qgraf's algorithm. It uses a naive entry-by-entry brute-force fill with post-hoc validation. qgraf uses 3-step row-by-row construction (Steps A/B/C in ALGORITHM.md §3) with integrated canonicalization: within-row ordering, cross-row ordering, loop budget tracking, and full permutation-class isomorph check. Our pairwise swap canonicalization misses isomorphisms, causing 2-loop overcounting (474 vs 465). This is NOT just a performance issue — it's an algorithmic correctness issue. Need to port qg21 properly: Steps A (external distribution) + B (self-loops) + C (row-by-row off-diagonal fill) with equivalence-class ordering. ~200 LOC Julia. Ref: refs/qgraf/ALGORITHM.md §3.2-3.5.

title: Implement ColourME (colour matrix elements)

close_reason: Architectural supersession: v2 does direct SU(N) colour algebra (colour_types.jl, colour_simplify.jl, colour_trace.jl) rather than FormCalc ColourME. Tests in test_colour.jl (27 tests). The FormCalc pattern may be reconsidered in the 'reimagining' phase if needed.

title: Implement amplitude squaring (SquaredME)
description: Reimplement FormCalc SquaredME logic. Separate squared amplitude into form factors and matrix elements. For fermions: M = Σ_i a_i F_i → |M|² = Σ_{ij} a_i* a_j Mat[F_i,F_j]. FormCalc's FF/FFC/Mat separation is the key pattern. Ref: FormCalc.m SquaredME section (~line 5031).
close_reason: Architectural supersession: v2 chose direct trace-product |M|² (spin_sum_amplitude_squared in src/v2/spin_sum.jl; evaluate_m_squared in cross_section.jl) rather than FormCalc FF/FFC/Mat separation. Bhabha |M|² test in test_bhabha.jl. The FormCalc pattern may be reconsidered in the 'reimagining' phase if genuinely needed.

title: Strip session/phase/spiral annotations from production source
description:
Anchor: src/v2/cross_section.jl:212-230; src/v2/qgraf/burnside_combine.jl:1; tests with `# Spiral N: ...` headers; HANDOFF.md (2186 LOC)
Reports: 07_smells.md §"Phase/Spiral/Session annotations as comments"; 02_torvalds.md "API/export surface" (extensive Session-27/29 essay block)

The codebase has 30+ `# Phase 18b-1 (Session 27)`, `# Spiral 4`, `# Session 29 attempted fix`, `# Phase 17c (Session 24)` comments interleaved with logic. Worst offender: cross_section.jl:212-230 — a 19-line essay describing "Strategy-A vs -B vs -C decision and fermion-line label matching as the gating issue" embedded INSIDE a function body. If a function needs a bedtime story to be understood, the function is too clever. Test files lead with `# Spiral N: <process>` followed by `@testset "Spiral N: <process>"` — once N spirals complete the labels confuse rather than clarify. HANDOFF.md is 2186 lines and growing — at this size it stops serving its handoff purpose.

Fix: one-touch sweep — move session/phase essays from production source into HANDOFF.md / DESIGN.md / beads issues. Source file headers should describe the PHYSICS rationale, not the calendar. Test `@testset` names should be physics-content (`@testset "Compton scattering tree |M|²"`) not ordinal (`@testset "Spiral 1: Compton scattering"`). Segment HANDOFF.md per-spiral with a top-level index. While there: delete "was @test_broken" comments (test_phase18b1_multi_orbit.jl:31, test_count_diagrams_qg21.jl:62) — git log already records that history.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/07_smells.md and 02_torvalds.md.

title: Specialise FourVector alias + drop _d() factory cosmetic
description:
Anchor: src/v2/pair.jl:32; src/v2/expr.jl:85-142
Reports: 08_julia_antipatterns.md (Refix #15 + Appendix F); 03_carmack.md (Pair aliases under-engineering)

Two cosmetic Julia-idiom cleanups:
- `FourVector = Union{Pair{LI,Mom}, Pair{Mom,LI}}` (pair.jl:32). The Pair constructor canonicalises so `Pair{Mom,LI}` is unreachable — every constructed FourVector is `Pair{LI,Mom}`. Tighten the alias to `Pair{LorentzIndex, Momentum}`; ~1 LOC, removes a dead union arm.
- `_d() = Dict{FactorKey, Coeff}` returns the *Type*, then `_d()(args...)` calls its constructor. 7 occurrences in expr.jl. Cute but unidiomatic — Julia readers expect `Dict{FactorKey, Coeff}(args...)` directly or `const _DCC = Dict{FactorKey, Coeff}` and `_DCC(args...)`. Pure cosmetic; ≤ 5 LOC.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/08_julia_antipatterns.md and 03_carmack.md.

title: Edge-case test gaps — Ward identity, crossing symmetry, IR poles
description:
Anchor: test/v2/ (new test_ward_identity.jl, test_crossing.jl, test_ir_poles.jl)
Reports: 09_test_coverage.md §"Edge-case coverage gaps" + recommendations #17,#18

Five physics-content edge cases are uncovered today: (a) Ward identity / gauge invariance — no test cross-checks Feynman gauge vs axial gauge for the same |M|² in qq̄→gg or qq̄→γγ. (b) Crossing symmetry — test_nlo_box_validation.jl checks Im(I) under crossing but no analogous test for tree (Bhabha s↔t → Møller; Compton s↔u → pair annihilation). (c) Bose / Fermi identical-particle symmetry — no test asserts antisymmetry of the Møller amplitude under (p1↔p2). (d) IR-finiteness — no tests labelled "IR" or "infrared". The Schwinger correction implicitly handles soft photon but there's no test that checks IR-finiteness of |M|² + soft-photon. (e) Dimensional regularization corners — no test asserts the ε⁻¹ pole structure of e.g. the unrenormalized self-energy.

Fix: write 3-5 targeted test files. Ward-identity is highest ROI (one `@test pipeline_feynman == pipeline_axial` after physical spin sum catches any future gauge regression). Crossing-symmetry tests via `expand_scalar_product` give symbolic equality via single relabel. ε-pole structure tests just check the `[ε⁻¹]` coefficient of the unrenormalized Σ matches Denner Eq (4.21) Δ. Total ~150 LOC across files.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/09_test_coverage.md.

title: Promote @warn-on-COLLIER-missing to @test_skip; document setup
description:
Anchor: test/v2/test_d0.jl:8-9, 47-105
Reports: 09_test_coverage.md §"@warn/@info census" + recommendation #10

When `refs/COLLIER/COLLIER-1.2.8/libcollier.so` is absent, the entire COLLIER block (test_d0.jl:47-105) is silently skipped after a single `@warn`. Risk: in a fresh checkout where COLLIER isn't built, `evaluate(D0(...))` gets a code-path-dependent fallback and the test suite passes without ever cross-validating COLLIER vs quadgk. Different developers running the suite get different assertion counts.

Fix: wire the warn into a `@test_skip` (or mark every assertion in the block with `if has_collier @test ... else @test_skip ... end`) so the skip is visible in the test output summary. File a CI-setup note (or beads-task) that COLLIER must be built for full coverage. ~10 LOC + a HANDOFF/CLAUDE.md sentence.

See reviews/ultra_2026-04-28/09_test_coverage.md.

title: Implement correct η-function corrections for Denner Spence decomposition
description: The current c0_analytical.jl omits the 't Hooft-Veltman η corrections (Eq 2.4) because the Denner C0p3 formula gives wrong results with them for spacelike kinematics. Root cause unknown — the Julia code matches the Fortran line-by-line but produces different numerical results. Either: (a) find the subtle bug in the η implementation, or (b) replace with FF library which avoids the issue entirely. This blocks correct analytical C₀ above threshold.
notes: DEFERRED: η correction bug is moot once we use LoopTools.jl ccall. The Denner C0p3 formula (C0func.F) is a backup checker, not the primary algorithm. Investigating its η issues is low priority while we have the robust FF library via ccall.

title: Implement FF library C₀ (ffxc0): correct analytical C₀ for all kinematic regions
description: The Denner C0p3 formula (C0func.F) is a backup double-checker in LoopTools, not the primary algorithm. It fails for certain kinematic regions (at/above threshold, degenerate momenta). The primary LoopTools algorithm is the FF library (ffxc0.F by van Oldenborgh), which uses a single-pass Spence decomposition without the 3-channel split and handles ALL cases correctly. Translate ffxc0.F (~500 LOC Fortran) to Julia. This replaces the current threshold-check workaround and quadgk fallback.
notes: DEFERRED: Native Julia reimplementation of ffxc0 is premature. The FF library is 3,474 lines of battle-tested Fortran with 30 years of numerical stability engineering. Strategy change: use LoopTools.jl ccall for C₀ NOW, reimplement in native Julia as a long-term project when we understand every edge case.

title: Epic: World-class diagram generation (Layer 3 rebuild)
description:
Full FeynArts-class diagram generation in pure Julia. NOT pragmatic — the best possible implementation.

PHASE 1: Topology enumeration via nauty/Traces (~200 LOC)
- Wrap nauty (C library, McKay & Piperno 2014) via ccall for canonical graph labeling
- Enumerate multigraphs with fixed external degree sequence + internal vertex valences
- Recursive insertion: split propagators, attach vertices, filter isomorphs via nauty hash
- Support tree (L=0), 1-loop (L=1), 2-loop (L=2) topology classes
- Symmetry factor = 1/|Aut(G)| computed by nauty as a byproduct
- Target: 2→6 tree topologies in <1s, 2→2 2-loop in <10s

PHASE 2: Field insertion via constraint propagation (~300 LOC)
- Model file defines allowed vertices as NTuple{k, FieldType} (k=3,4)
- CSP solver: assign field types to internal edges subject to vertex constraints
- Propagate: charge conservation, fermion number, colour flow at each vertex
- Fermion flow: trace continuous fermion lines, handle Majorana (sign from fermion loop)
- Ghost fields: Faddeev-Popov ghosts for non-abelian gauge theories
- Charge conjugation: identify C-related diagrams, combine
- Target: full SM field insertion for 2→4 in <5s

PHASE 3: Canonical form + amplitude mapping (~200 LOC)
- Map dressed diagram → amplitude expression (DiracChain + colour structure)
- Generalize build_amplitude from 2-vertex to N-vertex chains
- Handle arbitrary fermion line lengths (N vertices on a single line)
- Handle multi-gluon vertices (4-gluon from QCD Lagrangian)
- Symmetry factor applied automatically

PHASE 4: N-body phase space (~150 LOC)
- RAMBO algorithm (Kleiss, Stirling, Ellis 1986) for uniform N-body sampling
- VEGAS adaptive integration (Lepage 1978) for peaked integrands
- Multichannel integration: map each diagram topology to a phase space channel
- Soft/collinear singularity mapping (Catani-Seymour dipole subtraction)

REFERENCES:
- nauty/Traces: McKay & Piperno, J. Symb. Comput. 60 (2014) 94, https://pallini.di.uniroma1.it
- FeynArts: Hahn, Comp. Phys. Comm. 140 (2001) 153, hep-ph/0012260
- QGRAF: Nogueira, J. Comput. Phys. 105 (1993) 279
- RAMBO: Kleiss, Stirling, Ellis, Comp. Phys. Comm. 40 (1986) 359
- Catani-Seymour: Nucl. Phys. B485 (1997) 291, hep-ph/9605323
- Color decomposition: Maltoni, Stelzer, JHEP 0302 (2003) 027

ESTIMATED SCOPE: ~850 LOC across 4 phases. Spiral 12+ (after NLO validation complete).
NOT a priority now — current tree 2→2 + 1-loop box covers the active physics program.

User estimate critique: 'the estimates of time are so RIDICULOUSLY off' (original estimate was 18-22 sessions total).

BUG 1 ROOT CAUSE (qgraf qgen flavor-loop): Julia _qgen_recurse uses MULTISET matching (one sorted rule per fieldset) and assigns 'remaining' fields in sorted order to slots rdeg+1..vdeg. qgraf uses POSITIONAL matching with ALL distinct permutations of each vertex stored in dpntro/rotvpo (qrvi:22020-22090). For QED2 [e+,e-,γ] vertex, qgraf has 6 perms vs Julia's 1. When rdeg-prefix on a partially-assigned vertex requires a non-canonical slot order (e.g., [photon at slot 1, then e-,γ vs photon,γ,e-]), Julia generates only the sorted one, missing emissions that qgraf would emit. For ee→μμ 1L: Julia 16 vs qgraf 18 (deficit 2: 1 from penguin, 1 from box). Fix: enumerate distinct perms of remaining in _qgen_recurse + add multi-edge ordering check (qgen:13948-13954) and self-loop pair check (qgen:13921-13934). Source: refs/qgraf/v4.0.6/qgraf-4.0.6.dir/qgraf-4.0.6.f08:13880-13987.

FeynCalc 10.2.0 (dev, 22cc5e08) + wolframscript 1.13 + Mathematica 14.3 works. Load pattern (no paclet install needed):

  wolframscript -code 'PrependTo[$Path, "/home/tobiasosborne/Projects/Feynfeld.jl/refs/FeynCalc"]; Needs["FeynCalc`"]; ...'

Installation: /usr/local/Wolfram/Wolfram/14.3. User base: ~/.Wolfram. FeynCalc entry point: refs/FeynCalc/FeynCalc/FeynCalc.m.

Verified probes (Session 26, 2026-04-15):
- DiracTrace[GA[mu].GA[nu]] → 4*Pair[LorentzIndex[mu],LorentzIndex[nu]]
- DiracTrace[4 gammas] → canonical 3-term g·g·g form
- DiracTrace[4 gammas . GA[5]] → -4I*Eps[LorentzIndex[...]]
- PaVeReduce[B1[p2,m02,m12]] → full Denner Eq. (4.18) analytically
- Tdec[{{q,mu}},{p}] → tensor decomposition basis

$FCTraceNames shadow warning is cosmetic. InputForm emits Julia-mappable symbolic output. No JuliaForm exists — need hand-written translator per surface (~50 LOC per).

Phase 18a (Diagram→AlgSum bridge): 10 commits f4563bb..cd39db4. Symbolic equality solve_tree_pipeline ≡ solve_tree for ee→μμ tree massless. Pipeline: route_momenta → compute_amap → build_propagators/vertices/externals → walk_fermion_lines → emission_to_amplitude → solve_tree_pipeline. Boson Lorentz index naming divergence (:mu_l_<edge_id> pipeline vs :mu_<channel> handbuilt) currently masked but flagged for Phase 18b. Side fixes bundled into phase commits: MomentumSum/DiracChain/DiracExpr == and hash; qgen.jl vmap/lmap allocation n×n→n×MAX_V (latent self-loop bug).

Aggregate v2 test entry point: julia --project=. test/v2/runtests.jl. Single Julia process, loads FeynfeldX once, reuses JIT across all files, ~5 min, 605 assertions. Use THIS, not grind/run_v2_tests.sh (forks one Julia per file, ~300-500 MB each — contributed to Session 28.5 WSL OOM; root cause was concurrent project load, but the fork-per-file script compounds pressure). Qgraf tests (test/v2/qgraf/*.jl) NOT wired into runtests.jl — pre-existing coverage gap, see HANDOFF Session 28.
